rationale: The US-Iran conflict with Hormuz closure continues as before with no qualitative escalation this cycle — same baseline situation, so score holds but begins decay; all other headlines are irrelevant to the Polish user's daily life.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue, Hormuz closed
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchange strikes for a second consecutive day; Iran confirms hitting 18 US base targets and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed following a tanker strike with Indian sailors killed. This is a continuation of the already-known situation — no qualitative new escalation detected this cycle, so the baseline begins to decay slightly.

top_events.title: UK Defence Minister Healey resigns
top_events.summary: Britain's Defence Minister John Healey has resigned over disputes regarding defence spending levels. This is a continuation of the known situation from the previous cycle — ongoing NATO cohesion concern with indirect relevance to Poland's security posture, but no new escalation.

top_events.title: Earthquake in southern Philippines kills 37
top_events.summary: A powerful earthquake struck the southern Philippines, leaving at least 37 dead. Geographically distant from Poland with no economic or security linkage — minimal impact on the Polish user's daily life.
